channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Light Utility Helicopter Gets Teeth: LUH Armed Variant to Feature Rockets and Mistral AAMs
item.description: The Indian Army and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) have initiated the process to integrate armament on the Light Utility Helicopter (LUH), as reflected in the recent RFI for the LUH-RSH (Reconnaissance & Surveillance Helicopter) configuration. This marks a clear transition toward an armed variant of the LUH, tailored for light strike and self-defense missions rather than heavy attack roles.
item.dc:creator: RAUNAK KUNDE

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Set to Become AMCA Production Hub with Puttaparthi Mega Facility
item.description: The scale is consequential. With an estimated investment of around ?1 lakh crore, the Puttaparthi complex is being structured as the final assembly point for at least 140 AMCA airframes, alongside a dedicated flight-test range and an integrated township to support a long-duration program workforce. The site selection reflects a clear operational logic: proximity to the Aeronautical Development Agency in Bengaluru for design–production coupling, combined with access to relatively uncongested airspace and runway availability that can sustain high-tempo test campaigns—something increasingly difficult to guarantee at HAL Airport and Kempegowda International Airport.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Agni-VI Debate Sparks Narrative Battle as Pakistani Analysts Flag “Global Threat,” But Strategic Context Tells a Different Story
item.description: The core argument being pushed is that an Agni-VI with an estimated 12,000–16,000 km range represents a doctrinal shift from India’s stated policy of “credible minimum deterrence” toward global power projection. However, this interpretation requires closer scrutiny within the framework of nuclear strategy, force posture, and deterrence logic.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Indian Startup Olee.space Delivers Operational Directed Energy Weapon to Armed Forces, Claims Sub-$1 Per Shot Cost
item.description: The firm claims its DEW platform offers a disruptive cost advantage in counter-drone and short-range air defence roles, with a per-shot cost of less than one US dollar—significantly lower than traditional missile-based interceptors that can cost upwards of $150,000 per engagement. This cost asymmetry is increasingly relevant as militaries worldwide confront the proliferation of low-cost unmanned aerial systems that can overwhelm conventional air defence networks.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Japan Offers of Mogami-Class Frigate Design to India a major Indo-Pacific Push Strategy : Chinese Analyst
item.description: Each Mogami-class frigate is estimated to cost around US$500 million and is configured as a stealth-oriented, multi-mission warship equipped with anti-ship missiles, advanced air defence systems, and torpedoes. Designed with a strong emphasis on anti-submarine warfare (ASW), the platform is optimised to detect, track, and neutralise hostile submarines—an increasingly critical capability in the Indian Ocean Region, where undersea threats are expanding.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: India rejects Nepal’s objection to Kailash Manasarovar Yatra via Lipulekh Pass
item.description: New Delhi’s reaction came after the Nepal Foreign Ministry issued a statement objecting to India and China making preparations for the annual pilgrimage via Lipulekh Pass without consulting Kathmandu, which claims the region belongs to it.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Vizag-Bound Tanker With 46,313 Tonnes Of LPG Sails Safely Through Hormuz
item.description: The Marshall Islands-flagged ship has 20 crew members onboard, of whom as many as 18 are Indians, a ministry statement said. The ship, carrying over 45,000 tonnes of LPG, was earlier tracked moving into the Gulf of Oman after passing near Iran’s Larak and Qeshm islands on Saturday, according to ship-tracking data.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Ukrainian forces attack radiation monitoring lab of nuclear power plant
item.description: “The Ukrainian Armed Forces are posing a threat to the radiation safety of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant. This afternoon, the Ukrainian Armed Forces carried out a drone attack on the external radiation monitoring laboratory of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant,” the statement said.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Rajnath Singh To Inaugurate 3-Day Symposium On Military Tech Tomorrow
item.description: Themed ‘Raksha Triveni Sangam – Where Technology, Industry & Soldiering Converge’, the symposium in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, will be a three-day event being jointly organised by the Northern Command and Central Command of the Indian Army and the Society of Indian Defence Manufacturers (SIDM).
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Karachi Press Club Signal: Pakistan Openly Backing SFJ Push For India Referendum, Propaganda War
item.description: Officials say that this shows the open support that the Pakistan establishment offers the SFJ despite the outfit calling for violent attacks in India and also the assassination of Prime Minister Narendra Modi.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: India, Myanmar Discuss Strengthening Naval Cooperation, Defence Partnership
item.description: The two sides also spoke about issues related to enhancing training cooperation and future avenues of collaboration.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Chhattisgarh: Security Forces Seize Large Maoist Explosives Cache In Dantewada
item.description: One such operation in Dantewada district has yielded a significant breakthrough, with a massive cache of explosives and materials recovered from the dense forest area of village Todma under Barsur Police Station limits.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Archival Image Of Pak Satellite Shared As ‘Original’ To Whip Up Sentiments: Report
item.description: However, the circulation of an archival photo of the satellite, presented as an “after-launch” picture, dented the public mood with many questioning the sanctity and success of the satellite launch. The satellite, called PRSC-EO3, lifted off from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Centre in northern China and rode into the orbit aboard a Chinese Long March-6 rocket on April 25.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Dynamatic–Aerodata to Pitch ‘AeroForce X’ MALE UAV with Full Indian Design and Software Sovereignty for Tri-Service Tender
item.description: Dynamatic Technologies Limited, in partnership with Germany’s Aerodata AG, is positioning its jointly developed “AeroForce X” as a contender for the Indian Armed Forces’ requirement of 87 Medium Altitude Long Endurance (MALE) UAVs. Valued at roughly ?25,000 crore, the tri-service program is expected to become a defining competition for India’s next generation of ISR and armed UAV capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Flying Wedge Targets MALE Segment with 1-Ton Kaalbhairav UAV Capable of Armed ISR Missions at 20,000 ft
item.description: India’s emerging unmanned systems ecosystem continues to expand as startup Flying Wedge Defence and Aerospace has revealed plans to develop a 1-ton Medium Altitude Long Endurance (MALE) variant of its Kaalbhairav Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV). The company claims that this larger platform will be capable of operating at a ceiling altitude of up to 20,000 feet Above Mean Sea Level (AMSL), positioning it within a competitive bracket for tactical ISR and light strike roles.
item.dc:creator: admin
